paper.title_en: Primordial helium and effective neutrino species from CMB data: A Bayesian analysis of extended cosmological models
paper.abstract_en: We perform a Bayesian analysis of CMB data from the Planck and ACT experiments to constrain the primordial helium mass fraction YHe and the effective number of relativistic species Neff in extensions of the standard ΛCDM cosmological model. With Planck 2018 data alone we find no statistically significant departure from Big Bang Nucleosynthesis (BBN) consistency: YHe=0.240−0.013+0.013 (68% CL, free YHe) is compatible with the BBN-predicted value of YHeBBN=0.24672±0.00006 at the 0.4 σ level, and model selection yields a Bayes factor BΛCDM,ΛCDM+YHe≃10.7 in favor of the standard model. When ACT DR6 data are added, a mild  ∼ 2 σ tension emerges: the CMB damping tail prefers YHe=0.2256−0.011+0.0095, appreciably below the BBN-consistent value. In a ΛCDM+Neff extension the same preference manifests as Neff=2.75−0.15+0.13, about 2 σ below the standard value. A joint (YHe, Neff) extension recovers Neff compatible with 3.044 while maintaining the low-YHe direction. Bayesian model comparison consistently favors the simpler ΛCDM. We discuss possible systematic and physical interpretations of the mild tension, including degenerate BBN with a nonzero lepton asymmetry, a non-standard neutron lifetime, a transient change in Newton’s constant during BBN, and variations of other fundamental constants, evaluating their viability against correlated deuterium constraints.
